text_summary: Tisagenlecleucel (CTL019) demonstrated potent cytotoxicity against CD19+ Nalm-6 B-ALL cells in vitro. At E:T ratio of 10:1, specific lysis reached 92% after 24h co-culture, with near-complete elimination of target cells by 48h. Untransduced T-cells showed <5% killing. Data from preclinical characterization supporting ELIANA trial (Maude et al., NEJM 2018).
notes: PMID:29385376. 4-1BB costimulatory domain. Lentiviral vector.

text_summary: Tisagenlecleucel showed effective killing of CD19+ Raji Burkitt lymphoma cells in chromium release assay. At 10:1 E:T ratio, 78% specific lysis was observed at 4h. Dose-dependent killing confirmed across E:T ratios 1:1 to 20:1 with EC50 at approximately 3:1.
notes: Burkitt lymphoma model. 4h chromium release assay.

text_summary: Tisagenlecleucel CAR-T cells produced robust IFN-gamma secretion upon co-culture with CD19+ Nalm-6 cells. Mean IFN-gamma concentration of 12,450 pg/mL at 24h (E:T 1:1), compared to <50 pg/mL from untransduced controls. Cytokine production correlated with target cell killing efficiency.
notes: ELISA measurement. 24h co-culture supernatant.

text_summary: In the ELIANA trial, tisagenlecleucel demonstrated exceptional long-term persistence in pediatric B-ALL patients. CAR-T cells were detectable by qPCR in 68% of responders at 6 months post-infusion, with functional B-cell aplasia persisting as a surrogate marker. Median transgene persistence was 168 days. 4-1BB costimulation associated with superior T-cell longevity compared to CD28-based CARs (Maude et al., NEJM 2018).
notes: PMID:29385376. ELIANA trial. n=75 patients. qPCR detection in blood.

text_summary: In NSG mouse xenograft model bearing Nalm-6 B-ALL tumors, tisagenlecleucel achieved complete tumor clearance in 9/10 mice by day 28. Bioluminescence imaging confirmed >3-log reduction in tumor burden by day 14. Dose: 1e7 CAR-T cells IV. Survival extended beyond 60 days vs median 25 days for controls.
notes: NSG mouse model. 1e7 CAR-T IV dose. BLI imaging.

text_summary: Axicabtagene ciloleucel (KTE-C19) demonstrated rapid and potent cytotoxicity against CD19+ Raji lymphoma cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, >95% killing at 16h. CD28 costimulatory domain drives faster effector function compared to 4-1BB-based CARs. Supported ZUMA-1 trial design (Neelapu et al., NEJM 2017).
notes: PMID:29226797. CD28 costimulatory domain. Retroviral vector (gamma).

text_summary: Axicabtagene ciloleucel exhibited a rapid and intense cytokine release profile upon CD19+ target engagement. IFN-gamma peaked at 18,200 pg/mL (24h), IL-2 at 3,400 pg/mL, and TNF-alpha at 1,850 pg/mL. CD28-costimulated CAR-T cells showed 2-3x higher peak cytokine levels compared to 4-1BB constructs, correlating with higher CRS rates observed clinically in ZUMA-1.
notes: PMID:29226797. Multiplex cytokine analysis. Higher CRS risk with CD28.

text_summary: In ZUMA-1, axicabtagene ciloleucel CAR-T cell levels peaked at day 7-14 post-infusion (median 43 cells/uL) but declined rapidly. Only 34% of patients had detectable CAR-T cells at 6 months by flow cytometry. CD28 costimulation associated with rapid expansion but shorter persistence compared to 4-1BB CARs (Neelapu et al., NEJM 2017).
notes: PMID:29226797. ZUMA-1 trial. n=101 patients. Flow cytometry.

text_summary: Axicabtagene ciloleucel CAR-T cells from ZUMA-1 patients showed progressive exhaustion marker upregulation. PD-1 expression increased from 25% pre-infusion to 72% at day 7 peak expansion. LAG-3 co-expression on 41% of CAR-T cells at peak. TIM-3+PD-1+ double-positive T-cells correlated with reduced long-term persistence and eventual loss of response in relapsing patients.
notes: ZUMA-1 correlative analysis. CD28 CARs show faster exhaustion kinetics.

text_summary: In NSG mice bearing systemic Raji DLBCL tumors, axicabtagene ciloleucel at 5e6 CAR-T cells IV eradicated tumors in 8/10 mice by day 21. Rapid tumor clearance within first 7 days reflecting CD28-driven fast kinetics. However, late relapses observed in 2/8 initial responders by day 60, potentially due to CAR-T exhaustion.
notes: NSG mouse systemic tumor model. Late relapses in 25% of responders.

text_summary: Brexucabtagene autoleucel (KTE-X19) showed high cytotoxicity against CD19+ JeKo-1 mantle cell lymphoma cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 88% killing observed at 24h. Importantly, product manufactured with enriched T-cell process (T-cell selection prior to activation) showed more consistent killing across patient lots compared to standard leukapheresis-based manufacturing.
notes: ZUMA-2. MCL model. Enriched T-cell manufacturing process.

text_summary: In ZUMA-2 clinical trial for relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma, brexucabtagene autoleucel achieved 93% ORR (67% CR) in 68 evaluable patients. Median time to response was 1.0 month. CAR-T expansion peaked at day 8 with median 122 cells/uL. Durable responses observed with 57% remaining in response at 12 months (Wang et al., NEJM 2020).
notes: PMID:32023374. ZUMA-2 trial. n=68 MCL patients. CD28 costimulation.

text_summary: Lisocabtagene maraleucel (liso-cel) demonstrated potent and dose-dependent cytotoxicity against Nalm-6 B-ALL cells. At 10:1 E:T ratio, 94% killing at 24h. Unique feature: defined composition product with fixed CD4:CD8 ratio (1:1), resulting in more reproducible killing across patient lots compared to unselected products.
notes: TRANSFORM trial. Fixed CD4:CD8 1:1 ratio product. 4-1BB costim.

text_summary: Lisocabtagene maraleucel showed a favorable cytokine release profile compared to CD28-based CARs. IFN-gamma peaked at 8,900 pg/mL (24h co-culture with Raji cells), approximately 50% lower than axicabtagene. IL-6 levels were correspondingly lower, correlating with the reduced CRS rates observed in the TRANSCEND NHL 001 and TRANSFORM trials.
notes: 4-1BB costim associated with lower cytokine peaks and reduced CRS risk.

text_summary: Lisocabtagene maraleucel demonstrated favorable persistence kinetics in TRANSCEND NHL 001. CAR-T cells detectable by qPCR in 73% of patients at 6 months and 51% at 12 months. The 4-1BB costimulatory domain and defined CD4:CD8 composition contributed to sustained central memory T-cell phenotype. B-cell recovery observed in some patients with declining CAR-T levels.
notes: TRANSCEND NHL 001. 4-1BB + defined CD4:CD8 ratio enhances persistence.

text_summary: Idecabtagene vicleucel (ide-cel, bb2121) demonstrated BCMA-dependent cytotoxicity against RPMI-8226 multiple myeloma cells expressing high BCMA. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 85% killing at 24h. Minimal killing (<10%) of BCMA-negative K562 control cells confirmed target specificity. Killing efficiency correlated with surface BCMA density on target cells.
notes: PMID:33626253. KarMMa trial preclinical. 4-1BB costim. BCMA-dependent.

text_summary: Idecabtagene vicleucel showed strong killing of MM.1S multiple myeloma cells (moderate BCMA expression) with 72% lysis at 5:1 E:T ratio. Against U266 cells (low BCMA expression), killing dropped to 45%, demonstrating the dependence on antigen density. This BCMA density-dependent killing has implications for resistance via BCMA downregulation.
notes: Antigen density-dependent killing. Low-BCMA U266: only 45% killing.

text_summary: In the KarMMa trial, idecabtagene vicleucel achieved 73% ORR (33% CR/sCR) across all dose levels (150-450 x10e6 cells) in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients with >= 3 prior lines of therapy. At the highest dose (450 x10e6), ORR was 82% with 39% CR. Median duration of response was 10.7 months (Munshi et al., NEJM 2021).
notes: PMID:33626253. KarMMa trial. n=128. Dose-response relationship confirmed.

text_summary: Idecabtagene vicleucel CAR-T cells showed moderate persistence in KarMMa. Detectable in 45% of patients at 6 months by qPCR. Median peak expansion at day 11 (26 cells/uL). Patients achieving CR had significantly higher peak CAR-T expansion (median 54 vs 12 cells/uL, p<0.001). Loss of detectable CAR-T cells often preceded clinical relapse.
notes: KarMMa correlative. Higher expansion = better outcomes.

text_summary: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (cilta-cel) with its dual BCMA-binding VHH domain showed exceptional cytotoxicity against RPMI-8226 myeloma cells. At 1:1 E:T ratio, 89% killing at 24h — substantially higher than single-epitope BCMA CARs at the same ratio. The bivalent VHH design provides higher avidity binding that may overcome BCMA density heterogeneity.
notes: PMID:34534922. CARTITUDE-1. Dual VHH nanobody. Superior to single-chain.

text_summary: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel maintained high killing efficiency even against cells with low BCMA surface density. Against BCMA-low NCI-H929 cells, 76% lysis at 1:1 E:T ratio, compared to only 35% for single-epitope BCMA CARs. This enhanced activity against low-BCMA targets may explain the deeper and more durable responses observed in CARTITUDE-1 vs KarMMa.
notes: Low-BCMA model. Dual VHH overcomes antigen density limitation.

text_summary: In CARTITUDE-1, ciltacabtagene autoleucel achieved 98% ORR (83% sCR) in 97 heavily pretreated multiple myeloma patients (median 6 prior lines). Median time to first response: 1.0 month. At 27-month follow-up, median PFS was 27.7 months. Deepening responses over time with MRD-negativity in 92% of evaluable CR patients (Berdeja et al., Lancet 2021).
notes: PMID:34534922. CARTITUDE-1. n=97. Best-in-class BCMA CAR-T results.

text_summary: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel showed exceptional persistence in CARTITUDE-1. CAR-T cells detectable in 75% of patients at 12 months post-infusion by qPCR. Median peak expansion at day 13 (850 copies/ug DNA). The dual-VHH design combined with 4-1BB costimulation may contribute to sustained CAR-T persistence and deepening MRD-negative responses over time.
notes: CARTITUDE-1. Best persistence among BCMA CARs. Dual VHH + 4-1BB.

text_summary: Analysis of CD19 CAR-T relapse mechanisms across 145 patients from ELIANA and ZUMA-1 trials revealed antigen loss as the dominant resistance mechanism. CD19-negative relapse occurred in 28% of patients who initially responded then relapsed. Mechanisms include: CD19 exon 2 frameshift mutations (39%), alternative splicing skipping exon 2 (29%), and lineage switch to myeloid phenotype (15%). CD19 surface density below detection threshold in remaining cases.
notes: Multi-trial analysis. Antigen loss is primary resistance mechanism.

text_summary: Trogocytosis-mediated CD19 transfer was identified as a novel resistance mechanism. CD19 CAR-T cells actively strip CD19 from target cell surfaces during immune synapse formation. Flow cytometry showed 40-60% reduction in target cell CD19 MFI after 4h co-culture. Stripped CD19 on CAR-T surfaces triggered fratricide, reducing CAR-T persistence. This mechanism is antigen-independent and affects all CD19-targeted CARs.
notes: Hamieh et al., Nature 2019. PMID:30814727. Trogocytosis + fratricide.

text_summary: Head-to-head comparison of CD28 vs 4-1BB costimulatory domains in otherwise identical CD19 CARs. After repeated antigen stimulation (3 rounds, 5 days each), CD28-CARs showed 85% PD-1+/TIM-3+ double-positive (exhausted phenotype) vs 42% for 4-1BB CARs. CD28-CARs had higher peak killing but lost >70% of cytolytic activity by round 3. 4-1BB CARs maintained 65% of initial killing. Demonstrates the persistence vs potency tradeoff.
notes: Long et al., Nat Med 2015. CD28 = fast but exhaustible; 4-1BB = sustained.

text_summary: Soluble BCMA (sBCMA) acts as a decoy that inhibits BCMA-targeted CAR-T function. In vitro, physiological sBCMA concentrations (100-500 ng/mL, matching myeloma patient serum levels) reduced BCMA CAR-T killing by 30-45% in 24h cytotoxicity assays. At 500 ng/mL sBCMA, killing of RPMI-8226 dropped from 85% to 52%. Gamma-secretase inhibitors rescued CAR-T function by blocking BCMA shedding.
notes: sBCMA decoy mechanism. GSI co-administration rescues function.

text_summary: Anti-CD22 CAR-T cells (m971-based scFv, 4-1BB costimulation) showed moderate cytotoxicity against CD22+ Nalm-6 cells. At 10:1 E:T ratio, 65% killing at 24h — lower than CD19 CARs tested in parallel (92%). Sensitivity to CD22 surface density observed: cells with >5000 molecules/cell showed >80% killing while <1000 molecules/cell showed <30% killing. Supports use in CD19-negative relapse (Fry et al., Nat Med 2018).
notes: PMID:29155426. CD22 density-dependent. Rescue after CD19 loss.

text_summary: Bispecific CD19/CD22 dual-targeting CAR-T cells (bicistronic construct) showed synergistic killing in vitro. Against Nalm-6 cells (CD19+/CD22+), 96% killing at 5:1 E:T ratio. Against CD19-knockout cells (CD22+ only), maintained 72% killing. Against CD22-low cells (CD19+ only), maintained 90% killing. Dual targeting prevents single-antigen escape that drives 28% of CD19 CAR-T relapses.
notes: Bicistronic dual CAR. Prevents single-antigen escape.

text_summary: Flow cytometric analysis of tisagenlecleucel products revealed that central memory T-cell (Tcm) frequency in the infusion product predicted clinical response. Products with >40% CD8+ Tcm (CD45RO+/CCR7+/CD62L+) achieved 95% CR rate vs 58% for products with <20% Tcm. The 4-1BB costimulatory domain promoted Tcm differentiation during expansion. This finding informed manufacturing optimization for consistent Tcm enrichment.
notes: ELIANA correlative. Tcm frequency predicts response. Threshold: 40%.

text_summary: ZUMA-1 product characterization by flow cytometry showed variable CD4:CD8 ratios (range 0.3-8.0, median 1.4). Products with CD4:CD8 ratio between 0.8-2.0 showed highest ORR (87%) vs <60% for extreme ratios. CD8+ T-cells with CCR7-/CD45RA- effector memory phenotype were the dominant functional subset. Baseline patient T-cell fitness (pre-leukapheresis) was the strongest predictor of product quality.
notes: ZUMA-1 correlative. Product CD4:CD8 ratio affects outcomes.

text_summary: In orthotopic MM.1S multiple myeloma mouse model, ciltacabtagene autoleucel achieved complete tumor eradication in 10/10 mice at 1e6 CAR-T cell dose, compared to 6/10 for single-epitope BCMA CAR. BLI showed undetectable tumor signal by day 14 (cilta-cel) vs day 28 (single-epitope). The dual VHH nanobody architecture provides superior avidity that overcomes BCMA density heterogeneity within tumors.
notes: Dual VHH vs single-chain head-to-head. Cilta-cel clearly superior.

text_summary: Analysis of idecabtagene vicleucel products from KarMMa showed that pre-infusion T-cell exhaustion markers predicted clinical outcomes. Products with >50% PD-1+ CAR-T cells had ORR of 55% vs 84% for products with <30% PD-1+. Extended ex vivo culture (>10 days) was associated with increased exhaustion markers. Optimizing manufacturing to minimize culture time while achieving target cell dose is critical.
notes: KarMMa correlative. Product exhaustion predicts response. <30% PD-1 optimal.

text_summary: Comparison of cytokine cocktails for CAR-T expansion showed IL-7/IL-15 yielded superior products compared to IL-2 alone. After 10-day expansion, IL-7/IL-15 cultures had 3.2x more Tscm (stem cell memory) cells, 45% less exhaustion markers (PD-1/LAG-3), and 1.8x higher fold expansion. In subsequent cytotoxicity assay, IL-7/IL-15-expanded CAR-T cells maintained 82% killing through 5 rounds of serial stimulation vs 41% for IL-2-expanded.
notes: IL-7/IL-15 vs IL-2 manufacturing. Superior Tscm and less exhaustion.

text_summary: ELIANA trial (tisagenlecleucel in pediatric/young adult B-ALL): Overall remission rate 81% (61/75 patients) with 60% achieving MRD-negative CR by day 28. Median event-free survival 19.1 months. 77% of responders had grade 1-2 CRS, 48% had grade 3-4 CRS. Tocilizumab used in 48% of patients. ICANS in 40% (mostly grade 1-2). Overall survival at 12 months was 76% (Maude et al., NEJM 2018).
notes: PMID:29385376. ELIANA pivotal trial. n=75. Pediatric/YA B-ALL.

text_summary: ZUMA-1 trial (axicabtagene ciloleucel in DLBCL): ORR 82% with 54% CR in 101 patients with refractory large B-cell lymphoma. Median time to response: 1.0 month. At 27-month follow-up, 39% of patients maintained ongoing response. Grade >=3 CRS in 13%, grade >=3 neurological events in 28%. Peak CAR-T expansion correlated with both response and toxicity (Neelapu et al., NEJM 2017).
notes: PMID:29226797. ZUMA-1 pivotal trial. n=101. Refractory DLBCL.

text_summary: TRANSFORM trial (lisocabtagene maraleucel vs SOC in 2L LBCL): Liso-cel achieved significantly better EFS vs SOC (median 10.1 vs 2.3 months, HR 0.35, p<0.001). CR rate 74% vs 43%. First CAR-T approved in second-line setting. Favorable safety: grade >=3 CRS only 1%, grade >=3 ICANS 4%. Defined CD4:CD8 composition and 4-1BB costimulation contribute to safety profile (Kamdar et al., Lancet 2022).
notes: PMID:36517098. TRANSFORM trial. Liso-cel vs SOC in 2L LBCL.

text_summary: GPC3-targeted CAR-T cells (humanized GC33 scFv, 4-1BB/CD3z) showed effective killing of GPC3+ HepG2 hepatocellular carcinoma cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 73% specific lysis at 24h. Against GPC3-high Hep3B cells, killing increased to 81%. Demonstrates solid tumor targeting feasibility with GPC3, though in vivo efficacy limited by immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment.
notes: HCC solid tumor model. GPC3 targeting. TME remains challenge.

text_summary: HER2-targeted CAR-T cells with affinity-tuned scFv (4D5-5, reduced affinity for safety) selectively killed HER2-high SKOV3 ovarian cancer cells (78% at 5:1) while sparing HER2-low MCF10A normal breast epithelial cells (<15% killing). This affinity tuning approach addresses the on-target/off-tumor toxicity concern that led to a fatal outcome in an early HER2 CAR-T trial (Morgan et al., 2010).
notes: Affinity-tuned CAR for safety. Spares HER2-low normal tissue.

text_summary: Mesothelin-targeted CAR-T cells (SS1 scFv, 4-1BB costim) killed OVCAR-3 ovarian cancer cells at 68% lysis (10:1 E:T, 24h). Serial stimulation assay showed persistence advantage of 4-1BB costim: maintained 55% killing at round 3. In combination with PD-1 checkpoint blockade, killing increased to 82% and exhaustion markers reduced by 40%. Combination approaches promising for solid tumors.
notes: Solid tumor CAR-T. PD-1 blockade enhances function in TME.

text_summary: Biallelic BCMA loss identified as resistance mechanism in 4/14 (29%) patients relapsing after BCMA CAR-T therapy. Genomic analysis revealed hemizygous BCMA deletion (16p13.13) combined with nonsense or frameshift mutations on the remaining allele. Pre-existing subclonal BCMA-null cells expanded under selective pressure. Complete BCMA loss renders all BCMA-targeted therapies (CAR-T, BiTE, ADC) ineffective.
notes: Da Via et al., Nat Med 2021. PMID:33820986. Genomic BCMA loss.

text_summary: Lineage switch from lymphoid (B-ALL) to myeloid (AML) phenotype observed in 12/115 (10%) pediatric B-ALL patients treated with CD19 CAR-T across multi-institutional analysis. Switch characterized by loss of CD19/CD22/CD10 B-cell markers and gain of CD33/CD117/CD14 myeloid markers. KMT2A rearrangement was present in 9/12 cases (75%), suggesting pre-existing lineage plasticity. AML emerged at median 159 days post-infusion.
notes: Multi-center analysis. KMT2A-rearranged ALL at highest risk.

text_summary: Armored (4th generation) CAR-T cells engineered to constitutively secrete IL-18 showed enhanced anti-tumor activity in solid tumor models. Against pancreatic Panc02 tumors in immunocompetent mice, IL-18 armored CAR-T achieved 60% complete response vs 10% for standard 2nd-gen CAR-T. IL-18 reprogrammed the immunosuppressive tumor microenvironment by activating endogenous NK cells and M1 macrophages.
notes: 4th-gen armored CAR. IL-18 reshapes TME. Immunocompetent model.

text_summary: Manufacturing characterization of tisagenlecleucel showed median 42-fold expansion during 9-day culture period. Starting from leukapheresis product, T-cells activated with anti-CD3/CD28 beads in presence of IL-2. Transduction with lentiviral vector at MOI 5 achieved median 1.5 VCN (vector copies per diploid genome). Product viability >90% at harvest. Total manufacturing time: 22 days from leukapheresis to release.
notes: Novartis manufacturing. 22-day vein-to-vein. Lentiviral MOI 5.

text_summary: Axicabtagene ciloleucel manufacturing uses a rapid 6-day expansion protocol with gamma-retroviral vector. Median fold expansion: 25x. Anti-CD3 activation (no beads) with IL-2. Transduction efficiency: median 50% (range 28-74%). Faster manufacturing (6-7 days) than lentiviral products (9-12 days) enables more patients to receive treatment before disease progression. Failed manufacturing rate: 1% (vs ~8% for longer protocols).
notes: Kite/Gilead. 6-day rapid manufacturing. Gamma-retroviral vector.

text_summary: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (Carvykti) demonstrated exceptional serial killing capacity against BCMA+ RPMI-8226 multiple myeloma cells. In a serial killing assay with fresh target cells added every 24h, cilta-cel maintained >70% killing through 5 rounds (120h total), compared to <30% for single-epitope BCMA CARs by round 3. The dual VHH nanobody architecture provides sustained avidity-driven activation with reduced tonic signaling, preserving effector function across multiple rounds. Cumulative target cell elimination exceeded 15:1 effector-to-killed ratio (Berdeja et al., Lancet 2021).
notes: PMID:34534922. Serial killing 5 rounds. Dual VHH sustains function vs single-chain.

text_summary: Idecabtagene vicleucel (Abecma) 28-day in vitro persistence assay evaluated CAR-T survival and function without exogenous cytokines or restimulation. At day 28, 18% of initial CAR-T cells remained viable with 35% maintaining CAR surface expression by flow cytometry. Surviving cells were enriched for central memory phenotype (CD45RO+/CCR7+/CD62L+, 62% of viable). Functional assessment at day 28 showed reduced but measurable cytotoxicity: 38% killing of RPMI-8226 at 5:1 E:T, compared to 85% at day 0. 4-1BB costimulation supported longer survival vs CD28 constructs (undetectable by day 14).
notes: 28-day in vitro persistence without cytokines. 4-1BB costim advantage. Tcm enrichment.

text_summary: Anti-CD22 CAR-T cells using the m971 scFv (high-affinity, 4-1BB/CD3z signaling) demonstrated robust cytotoxicity against CD22-high NALM-6-CD22+ cells engineered to overexpress CD22. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 82% specific lysis at 24h, significantly higher than against parental NALM-6 (65% at 10:1). Flow cytometry confirmed >10,000 CD22 molecules/cell on NALM-6-CD22+ vs ~3,000 on parental line. IFN-gamma secretion was 3.2-fold higher against CD22-high targets. These data support CD22 CAR-T as a rescue strategy for CD19-negative relapse, particularly when CD22 expression is preserved above the density threshold (Fry et al., Nat Med 2018).
notes: PMID:29155426. m971 scFv. CD22-high engineered line. Rescue for CD19-neg relapse.

text_summary: Mesothelin-targeted CAR-T cells (mesoCAR-T, SS1 scFv with 4-1BB costimulation) were evaluated in a transwell migration assay to assess chemotaxis toward solid tumors. MesoCAR-T cells demonstrated 3.8-fold greater migration through 8um pore transwell membranes toward mesothelin-expressing OVCAR-3 conditioned media compared to untransduced T-cells over 4 hours. Migration was CXCL12/CXCR4-dependent, with AMD3100 blockade reducing migration by 65%. Armored mesoCAR-T co-expressing CXCR4 showed 6.2-fold enhanced migration. Transwell migration correlated with in vivo tumor infiltration in subsequent xenograft studies.
notes: Transwell 8um pore. CXCL12/CXCR4 axis. CXCR4 armoring enhances to 6.2-fold.

text_summary: GPC3-targeted CAR-T cells (humanized GC33 scFv, 4-1BB/CD3z) were evaluated in an orthotopic HepG2 hepatocellular carcinoma xenograft model in NSG mice. At a dose of 5e6 CAR-T cells IV, significant tumor growth inhibition was observed with 55% reduction in tumor volume at day 28 (p<0.01 vs untransduced). Complete response in 3/10 mice. Tumor-infiltrating CAR-T analysis at day 21 showed 15% of intratumoral CD3+ cells were CAR+, with upregulation of PD-1 (68%) and TIM-3 (45%) indicating TME-driven exhaustion. Combination with anti-PD-1 improved CR to 6/10 mice.
notes: NSG mouse orthotopic HCC. TME exhaustion limits efficacy. Anti-PD-1 combination improves CR.

text_summary: HER2-targeted CAR-T cells with affinity-tuned scFv (4D5-5) were assessed for trafficking and tumor infiltration in a 4T1 orthotopic breast cancer model in BALB/c mice. Bioluminescence imaging of luciferase-labeled CAR-T cells showed initial lung accumulation (0-6h), followed by redistribution to tumor by 24-48h. Peak intratumoral CAR-T density at day 5 with 2.3% of injected dose per gram tumor tissue. Tumor-infiltrating CAR-T cells showed effector phenotype (CD44hi/CD62Llo) but rapid PD-1 upregulation (78% by day 7). Serial peripheral blood sampling confirmed persistence with detectable circulating CAR-T through day 21.
notes: Syngeneic 4T1 model. BLI trafficking. Lung sequestration then tumor homing. TME exhaustion.

text_summary: CD19 CAR-T cells (FMC63 scFv, 4-1BB costimulation) were subjected to chronic antigen stimulation using irradiated CD19+ K562 feeder cells replenished every 48h for 14 days to model sustained tumor antigen exposure. By day 14, CAR-T cells exhibited progressive exhaustion: PD-1 increased from 22% to 89%, LAG-3 from 8% to 67%, TIM-3 from 15% to 74%. Triple-positive (PD-1+/LAG-3+/TIM-3+) exhausted T-cells comprised 52% of the population. Cytotoxicity against fresh Nalm-6 targets declined from 94% (day 0) to 28% (day 14). TOX transcription factor expression, a master regulator of exhaustion, increased 4.8-fold by day 14.
notes: 14-day chronic stimulation model. TOX upregulation 4.8x. 4-1BB delays but does not prevent exhaustion.

text_summary: Ciltacabtagene autoleucel cytokine secretion profile was characterized by 42-plex Luminex panel upon co-culture with RPMI-8226 BCMA+ myeloma cells at 1:1 E:T ratio. At 24h: IFN-gamma 14,800 pg/mL, TNF-alpha 2,100 pg/mL, IL-2 890 pg/mL, GM-CSF 3,200 pg/mL, IL-6 450 pg/mL, IL-10 380 pg/mL. Notably, the IFN-gamma:IL-6 ratio of 33:1 was more favorable than single-epitope BCMA CARs (ratio 12:1), suggesting more directed cytotoxicity with less bystander inflammation. Granzyme B release was 8,400 pg/mL, confirming granule-mediated killing as the primary effector mechanism.
notes: 42-plex Luminex. Favorable IFN-gamma:IL-6 ratio. Granzyme B dominant killing.

text_summary: MCARH109, an FcRH5-targeted CAR-T construct (anti-FcRH5 scFv, 4-1BB/CD3z), demonstrated potent cytotoxicity against MM.1S multiple myeloma cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 79% specific lysis at 24h. FcRH5 (Fc receptor-like protein 5, also known as IRTA2) is expressed on >95% of myeloma cells and is not cleaved from the surface (unlike BCMA), providing a stable target. Against BCMA-knockout MM.1S cells, MCARH109 maintained full killing (81%), while BCMA CARs showed <10% killing, confirming target independence. Phase 1 clinical trial (NCT03410598) showed 57% ORR in BCMA-refractory myeloma patients.
notes: MCARH109. FcRH5 not shed (unlike BCMA). Effective in BCMA-refractory. NCT03410598.

text_summary: CD70-targeted CAR-T cells (anti-CD70 scFv, 4-1BB/CD3z) showed potent cytotoxicity against CD70+ MOLM-13 acute myeloid leukemia cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 84% killing at 24h. CD70 is expressed on AML blasts (60-70% of cases) and leukemic stem cells (LSC) but has limited expression on normal hematopoietic stem cells, providing a favorable therapeutic window. Against CD34+CD38- LSC-enriched primary AML samples, 68% killing at 10:1 was observed. Minimal killing (<12%) of normal CD34+ HSPCs from healthy donors confirmed the preferential targeting of malignant over normal cells.
notes: AML model. CD70 on blasts + LSCs. Spares normal HSPCs. Therapeutic window for AML.

text_summary: A bivalent bispecific CAR-T construct targeting both CD19 and CD22 from a single chimeric receptor (tandem scFv loop design) was tested against NALM-6 B-ALL cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, the bispecific CAR achieved 94% killing at 24h, comparable to single-target CD19 CAR (92%) and superior to single-target CD22 CAR (65%). Against isogenic CD19-KO NALM-6 (CD22 only), bispecific maintained 74% killing. Against CD22-KO (CD19 only), 88% killing. Simultaneous engagement of both antigens synergistically enhanced immune synapse formation (2.4x larger synaptic area by imaging). Phase 1 data (NCT03289455) showed 88% CR in CD19-neg relapse patients.
notes: Tandem scFv bispecific. Synergistic synapse. Prevents single-antigen escape. NCT03289455.

text_summary: CAR-T cell proliferative capacity after chronic antigen stimulation was assessed using CFSE dilution and cell counting. CD19 CAR-T cells (FMC63 scFv) were repeatedly stimulated with irradiated CD19+ K562 cells every 3 days for 5 rounds (15 days total). IL-7/IL-15-expanded products maintained 4.2-fold expansion per round through round 5, while IL-2-expanded products showed proliferative arrest by round 3 (<1.5-fold). CFSE dilution confirmed active cell division in IL-7/IL-15 products with 92% of cells dividing at round 5 vs 31% for IL-2 products. Ki-67 positivity was 78% vs 22% respectively.
key_metric: fold expansion per round at round 5
notes: CFSE proliferation assay. IL-7/IL-15 maintains proliferation vs IL-2 arrest at round 3.

text_summary: TROP2-targeted CAR-T cells (anti-TROP2 scFv derived from sacituzumab govitecan antibody, 4-1BB/CD3z) demonstrated cytotoxicity against TROP2-high MDA-MB-231 triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) cells. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 71% killing at 24h. TROP2 is overexpressed in >80% of TNBC and multiple epithelial carcinomas. Against TROP2-low MCF-7 cells, killing was reduced to 32%, confirming antigen density-dependent activity. In a 3D spheroid model, TROP2 CAR-T infiltrated and disrupted MDA-MB-231 spheroids with 58% volume reduction by day 3, though residual viable tumor cells persisted in the spheroid core.
notes: TNBC solid tumor model. TROP2 overexpressed in >80% TNBC. 3D spheroid confirms infiltration.

text_summary: FLT3-targeted CAR-T cells (anti-FLT3 scFv, 4-1BB/CD3z) showed potent and specific cytotoxicity against FLT3+ MV4-11 AML cells harboring the FLT3-ITD mutation. At 5:1 E:T ratio, 86% killing at 24h. Against FLT3-wild type THP-1 cells (lower surface FLT3), 61% killing was observed. FLT3 is expressed on AML blasts in ~95% of cases with variable density. Serial killing assay showed maintained function through 3 rounds (>60% killing per round). Against normal CD34+ cord blood HSPCs, 28% killing was observed, raising concerns about myelosuppressive on-target/off-tumor effects requiring careful dose optimization.
notes: AML FLT3-ITD model. 95% AML express FLT3. On-target/off-tumor on HSPCs concerning.

text_summary: EpCAM-targeted CAR-T cells (anti-EpCAM scFv, 4-1BB/CD3z, armored with IL-15 co-expression) were evaluated in NSG mice bearing subcutaneous HT-29 colon carcinoma xenografts. At a dose of 5e6 CAR-T cells IV, significant tumor growth delay was observed with 62% reduction in tumor volume at day 28 vs untransduced controls (p<0.005). Complete response in 4/10 mice. IL-15-armored construct outperformed non-armored EpCAM CAR (32% tumor reduction, 1/10 CR). Tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte analysis showed 3.1x higher CAR-T density in IL-15-armored tumors with preserved Tscm phenotype and reduced PD-1 expression (38% vs 72%).
notes: NSG mouse colon cancer. IL-15 armored construct superior. Preserved Tscm in TME.
